record_identity.title: Predictive care estimate converted into essential-service denial

summary: A predictive risk, utilisation, or care-duration estimate is treated as an entitlement rule that denies, curtails, or burdens medically necessary care without adequate clinical review, transparency, and appeal.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: CAM needs a firm boundary between decision support and authority over essential services. Statistical estimates cannot silently displace individual assessment, clinical responsibility, or meaningful contestability.

source_records.relevance_note: Supports the recurring mechanism represented by this failure mode; underlying claims remain bounded by the incident record and its cited reports.

primary_artefact_access.access_method: Direct text review of the public AI Incident Database incident record

Incident inclusion supports pattern analysis but does not determine legal liability or final factual truth.

source_records.interpretive_reliance: Relied on as a structured secondary incident record for mechanism-level corroboration, not as an independent adjudication of every reported fact.

system_context.system_type: healthcare prediction and benefits decision support
system_context.embodiment_status: digital system with real-world institutional effects
system_context.deployment_context: predictive scores used around access to healthcare or medication
system_context.user_role: affected person, operator, or institutional decision-maker
system_context.affected_population: patients, disabled people, older people, and people requiring pain treatment

jurisdictional_context.cross_border_relevance: high because predictive healthcare allocation systems are widely transferable
jurisdictional_context.public_interest_relevance: very high — health, life, dignity, equality, and essential-service access

failure_mode_definition: A population-derived prediction or opaque risk score is converted into a presumptive or binding individual care entitlement, overriding or chilling clinician-led assessment without adequate explanation, exception handling, and appeal.
failure_threshold: Met when an automated estimate materially drives denial, early termination, restriction, or deterrence of medically indicated care and individual clinical evidence cannot reliably override the output through a timely, intelligible process.

failure_classification.recurrence_pattern: multiple reported incidents across deployments or organisations
failure_classification.taxonomy_reference: CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 Appendix B — Runtime & Governance Failure Taxonomy
failure_classification.persistence: persists while probabilistic outputs can trigger consequential authority without effective independent verification, appeal, or bounded execution
failure_classification.reproducibility: mechanism corroborated across the cited incident records; individual facts remain source-bounded
failure_classification.visibility: denial is visible to the patient, while the predictive basis, override rate, and institutional incentives may remain hidden

triage.recommended_next_step: Review CAM healthcare, Identity, Operations, and economic-legitimacy controls for a decision-support/entitlement boundary, clinically accountable override, explanation, audit, and rapid appeal.

cam_internal.governance_layer: essential-service governance / predictive classification / clinical accountability / appeal
cam_internal.proposal_needed: to be assessed after corpus review
cam_internal.routing_note: Treat as external ecosystem evidence requiring CAM corpus crosswalk; do not infer implementation or repair from record creation.
cam_internal.validator_or_automation_impact: potential monitoring of automated denial rates, overrides, explanations, and appeals

ecosystem_status.basis: Multiple public incident records evidence the mechanism or materially similar variants.

repair_status.monitoring_status: active incident monitoring and corpus crosswalk pending
repair_status.verification_note: No ecosystem-wide repair or runtime conformance was verified in this pass.
remaining_gaps: Independent verification of the underlying incident claims remains incomplete.
A current Caelestis control and implementation crosswalk has not yet been completed.
External adoption and runtime conformance remain unknown.

corpus_coverage.coverage_summary: The record has been classified and routed, but a deep current-corpus review is required before claiming direct, partial, or absent CAM coverage.
remaining_gaps: Review current Identity, Security, Operations, Continuity, Observability, and applicable sectoral doctrine against this mechanism.
Do not create PATCH-0025 without the separate Caelestis review-document deep dive requested by the Human Governance Editor.
